author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[412]): QA Langlands Global Functional Equation — closes [403]→[412] ladder
N=5^8=390625; d=4; w=k−1=1; L_∞=Γ_ℝ(s+1/2)²Γ_ℝ(s+3/2)² from parallel weight 2
over 2 real embeddings of ℚ(√5). Gamma shifts {1/2,1/2,3/2,3/2} as Fraction (not float
— Theorem NT). Complementarity μ+(2−μ)=2 certifies archimedean self-duality. Float
observer projections (ε,Γ values,L(1/2)) correctly excluded from QA layer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[411]): QA Langlands Ramified p=5 — P_5^{ram}=1, completes AI Euler product
Conductor exponent n=3≥2 at 𝔭₅ → supercuspidal local type → trivial L-factor.
Artin induction formula a_5=[F₅:ℚ₅]·n+dim·f(F₅/ℚ₅)=2·3+2·1=8 (integer only).
Together [404]+[409]+[411] classifies ALL primes: split {p%5∈1,4}, inert {2,3},
ramified {0}=p=5. With [410] the full ζ_{ℚ(√5)} Euler structure is certified.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[410]): QA Langlands Dedekind Zeta Factorization — ζ_{ℚ(√5)}=ζ·L(s,χ₅)
Certifies the Euler product factorization of the Dedekind zeta of F=ℚ(√5).
Kronecker symbol χ₅(p)=p%5 arithmetic classifies 44 primes (22 split, 22 inert).
Split local numerator [1,−2,1]=(1-Y)², inert [1,0,−1]=1-Y². Norm product
∏N(𝔭)=p²=p^[F:ℚ] ties directly to a₄(P_p)=p² in AI Euler factors [404]+[409].
Synthesizes the full unramified prime classification into ζ_F structure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[409]): QA Langlands AI Inert Prime Euler Factor — P_p=1+p²Y⁴
GL₄/ℚ AI Euler factor at primes p ≡ 2,3 mod 5 (inert in ℚ(√5)/ℚ).
Completes the unramified prime classification for AI(f).

P_p^{inert}(Y) = 1 + p²·Y⁴   (all middle coefficients zero)

Three-step algebraic derivation:
  (1) CM vanishing: ord_{(ℤ/5ℤ)^×}(p)=4 → (p) inert in ℚ(ζ₅)/ℚ(√5) → a_𝔭=0
  (2) GL₂/F local: Q_𝔭(Z)=1+p²Z² (Z=p^{-2s})
  (3) AI induction at inert p: L_p(Y,AI)=L_𝔭(Y²,f)=1+p²Y⁴

Structural gap (C4): naive T=N=0 in split formula → (1+pY²)²=1+2pY²+p²Y⁴;
correct inert formula has a₂=0 not 2p. Gap=2p for every inert prime.

Four checks, 22 inert primes p ≤ 200: all PASS.
Together [404]+[409] classify all unramified GL₄/ℚ AI Euler factors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[408]): QA Langlands Sym² Full GL₆/Q Euler Factor — V_p=(1−pY)²·Σ_p
Full Sym²/Q Euler polynomial at split primes for f=2.2.5.1-125.1-a.
Completes the Sym² branch of the Langlands ladder (parallel to [406] ∧²).

V_p(Y) = (1−pY)² · Σ_p(Y)   [degree-6 GL₆/Q integer polynomial]

Convolution: V_p[k] = Σ_p[k] − 2p·Σ_p[k−1] + p²·Σ_p[k−2]
The (1−pY)² factor is the CM trivial component (norm=p at each 𝔭, 𝔭̄).

Four checks, 22 split primes p≤500:
  C1: all 7 integer coefficients ∈ ℤ — 22/22 PASS
  C2: weight-2 palindrome a₆=p⁶, a₅=p⁴a₁, a₄=p²a₂ — 22/22 PASS
  C3: V_p = (1−pY)²·Σ_p by dual convolution paths — 22/22 PASS
  C4: GL₆ Ramanujan all 6 roots |root|=p⁻¹ — 22/22 PASS

Ladder: [403]→[404]→[407]→[408]; parallel: [405]→[406].
[406]+[408] together certify both tensor branches at split primes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[407]): QA Langlands Sym² Reduced Factor — GL₃/F Euler Component
Sym²(f) GL₃/F reduced factor Σ_p for f=2.2.5.1-125.1-a at split primes.

Σ_p(Y) = 1 − S·Y + (Q+2p²)·Y² − p²S·Y³ + p⁴·Y⁴
  S = T²−2N−4p   (sum c₁+c₂ where cᵢ = σᵢ(a_p)²−2p)
  Q = (N+2p)²−2p·T²   (product c₁c₂)

Four checks, 22 split primes p≤500:
  C1: integer S, Q+2p², p²S, p⁴ — 22/22 PASS
  C2: palindrome a₃=p²a₁, a₄=p⁴ — 22/22 PASS
  C3: Vieta derivation c₁+c₂=S, c₁c₂=Q — 22/22 PASS
  C4: Sym² Ramanujan cᵢ²<4p² (from [403]) → complex roots |root|=p⁻¹ — 22/22 PASS

Theorem NT: cᵢ=σᵢ(a_p)²−2p are observer projections only.
Ladder branch: [403]→[404]→[407]; next: [408] = (1−pY)²·Σ_p.
Primary sources: Shimura 1975, Gelbart-Jacquet 1978, Kim-Shahidi 2002.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[406]): QA Langlands GL6 Exterior Square — ∧²(AI(f)) Euler Factor
Cert [406] closes the GL6 rung of the Langlands ladder. By the decomposition
∧²(V₁⊕V₂) = ∧²V₁ ⊕ (V₁⊗V₂) ⊕ ∧²V₂, the exterior square of AI_{Q(√5)/Q}(f)
at split p is W_p = (1−pY)² · R_p(Y), giving a degree-6 polynomial with
explicit integer coefficients:

  W_p(Y) = 1 − (N+2p)Y + p(T²−p)Y²
           − 2p²(T²−N−2p)Y³
           + p³(T²−p)Y⁴ − p⁴(N+2p)Y⁵ + p⁶Y⁶

Four checks (all 22 split primes p≤500):
  C1: All 7 coefficients in Z
  C2: Weight-2 palindrome a5=p^4*a1, a4=p^2*a2, a6=p^6
  C3: Middle a3 divisible by 2p² (double-multiplicity signature)
  C4: W_p = (1−pY)²·R_p(Y) direct multiplication match

SHA-256: 02bc6005a2a7f3202cd474830392135610eed6ca3a32d509bd100e026a483a92

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[405]): QA Langlands GL4 Rankin-Selberg — Tensor Product Local Factor
Cert [405] derives the GL4 tensor product Euler polynomial at split primes
from the two GL2 local components of AI_{Q(sqrt5)/Q}(f). By Vieta:
sigma_1(a_p)*sigma_2(a_p) = N and sigma_1^2+sigma_2^2 = T^2-2N, so the
cross-product Q_2(alpha_1*Y)*Q_2(alpha_2*Y) collapses to integer coefficients:
R_p(Y) = 1 - NY + p(T^2-2N-2p)Y^2 - p^2*N*Y^3 + p^4*Y^4.

Three checks (22 split primes each):
  C1: All coefficients integer (immediate from T,N in Z)
  C2: Weight-2 palindrome a3=p^2*a1 and a4=p^4
  C3: Vieta derivation verified via float observer projections

Relation: wedge2(AI(f))_p = (1-pY)^2 * R_p(Y) — cert [405] isolates R_p.

SHA-256: 9a7e6a85cf1a5f8c33b16a7a873a8c1b3dca2e84ebc56cb4132a2244ffae0d3b

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[404]): QA Langlands GL4 Induction — AI_{Q(sqrt5)/Q}(f) Euler Factors
Cert [404] is the next rung above [403] on the Langlands ladder. For the CM
Hilbert modular form 2.2.5.1-125.1-a, the GL4/Q automorphic induction
AI_{Q(sqrt5)/Q}(f) has Euler polynomial P_p(Y) = (1-a_p Y+pY^2)(1-sigma_F(a_p)Y+pY^2)
= 1-TY+(N+2p)Y^2-pTY^3+p^2 Y^4 at every split prime.

Four checks, all 22 split primes p<=500:
  C1: T = Tr(a_p) ≡ -1 mod 5 (Frobenius conductor λ³ forces Tr_{K/Q}(π)≡4 mod 5)
  C2: Z[phi]-factorization gives integer GL4 coefficients
  C3: Palindrome a4=p^2, a3=p·a1 (functional equation purity)
  C4: GL4 Ramanujan — all 4 roots have |root|=p^{-1/2} (inherited from [403])

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[403]): Universal Pell characterization for all CM eigenvalue classes
Extends cert [403] from 5 LMFDB primes to all 22 split primes p<=500 for the
CM Hilbert modular form 2.2.5.1-125.1-a over F=Q(sqrt(5)).

New check C6 (Universal Pell): for every split prime with a_p=(u,v) in Z[phi],
  M^2 - 20k^2 = T^2 * D
where T=Tr(a_p)=2u+v, D=disc(a_p/Q)=T^2-4N(a_p), M=8p-(T^2+D)/2.

Disc classes: D = m^2 * 125 for m in {1,4,5} only (D in {125, 2000, 3125}).
Pell discriminant 20=4*F5 and stepping unit (9,2) solving X^2-20Y^2=1 are
universal across all eigenvalue classes. Spurious eigenvalues for p=311,461
from prior bm03phaz2 search corrected via Pell validity filter.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
fix(cert:[403]): correct Frobenius eigenvalues for p=41,61 via Z[zeta5] search
The Z[zeta5] mod 5^3 Hecke character computation (N=p^2, |sigma_k|=sqrt(p),
v_{zeta5-1}(pi-1)>=3) gives:
  a41 = 7 - 5*phi  [was 6-5*phi, a transcription error]
  a61 = 2 - 5*phi  [was -3+10*phi, a transcription error]

Key algebraic fact uncovered: sigma_F(a11) = sigma_F(-3+5*phi) = 2-5*phi = a61.
The generator eigenvalue's Galois conjugate reappears exactly at p=61.

Frobenius witnesses:
  p=41: pi = 2 - 3*zeta5 - 2*zeta5^2 + 4*zeta5^3
  p=61: pi = -2*zeta5 - 3*zeta5^2 + 6*zeta5^3

All 5 checks PASS with corrected norms N={11:-31, 31:-1, 41:-11, 61:-31, 71:59}.
Expected hash refreshed to 59181f66...

LMFDB 2.2.5.1-125.1-a identified as the CM Hilbert modular form induced
by K=Q(zeta_5)/F=Q(sqrt(5)). Five algebraic checks, all PASS:
- C1: level 125 = 5^3 = disc(Q(zeta_5)/Q)
- C2: e = 5*phi-3 in Z[phi] satisfies e^2+e-31=0

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[398]): QA Five Families Complete Partition
Computational proof of Theorem 2 of the Pythagorean Five Families paper.

CLAIM: The five Fibonacci-like seeds (1,1),(2,1),(3,1),(3,3),(9,9) under
digital-root T-step T(b,e)=(e,dr(b+e)) produce pairwise-disjoint orbits
of sizes 24+24+24+8+1=81=9^2 covering all {1,...,9}^2.

  (C1) orbit closure:     each seed returns to start in exactly 24/24/24/8/1 steps
  (C2) no premature end:  minimum period = stated period (exact)
  (C3) pairwise disjoint: all 10 family intersections empty
  (C4) complete coverage: union = {1,...,9}^2
  (C5) table match:       all 81 cells match paper Table 1

QA orbit shadow:
  Cosmos (72)    = Fibonacci(24) + Lucas(24) + Phibonacci(24)
  Satellite (8)  = Tribonacci(8)
  Singularity(1) = Ninbonacci(1)

This cert is the executable form of the paper's computational proof
and can be cited directly from §7 (Computational Verification).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[397]): QA CM Relative Norm and Tower Law
CLAIM: For pi=a+b*zeta_5 with N_{K/Q}(pi)=p, K=Q(zeta_5), F=Q(sqrt(5)):

  (C1) N_{K/F}(a+b*zeta_5) = (a^2-ab+b^2) + ab*phi   [relative norm, exact Z[phi]]
  (C2) N_{F/Q}(N_{K/F}(pi)) = p                        [tower law identity]
  (C3) N_{K/F}(pi) * sigma_F(N_{K/F}(pi)) = p          [CM norm factoring]
  (C4) min poly X^2 - Tr_{K/F}*X + N_{K/F}  in Z[phi][X]
  (C5) disc = -b^2*(2+phi)  TOTALLY NEGATIVE for b != 0
       N_{F/Q}(disc) = 5*b^4 > 0

Totally-negative discriminant iff Weil bound holds at every real place
of Q(sqrt(5)) — the degree-4/CM analog of cert [395]'s sign flip.

Langlands chain complete at degree-4:
  [394] GL_1  (5/p) sigma-orbit
  [395] GL_2  Weil bound, discriminant sign flip
  [396] K/F   Z[zeta_5] infrastructure
  [397] CM    relative norm + totally-negative discriminant  <-- this

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[395]): QA Weil Bound from Cassini — discriminant sign flip GL_1→GL_2
Cert [395]: |a_f(𝔭)|² < 4·N(𝔭) for all 34 prime ideals with N(𝔭) ≤ 151.

Structural insight: the Fibonacci Frobenius (weight-0, Cassini det=-1) has
characteristic polynomial x²-L_p·x-1 with discriminant Δ_Fib = L_p²+4 > 0
(real eigenvalues). The GL_2 Frobenius (weight-2) has char poly x²-a_f·x+p
with Δ_GL2 = a_f²-4p < 0 (complex eigenvalues). The Weil bound is equivalent
to Δ_GL2 ≤ 0. The weight shift det=-1 → det=p flips the discriminant sign.
Cassini invariant is the weight-0 analogue of the Weil bound.

5/5 checks PASS (C1 Weil, C2 strict/non-CM, C3 Fib disc, C4 GL2 disc, C5 Cassini).
Extends [391] (Cassini), [394] (GL_1 Frobenius), [390] (LMFDB data).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[394]): QA Fibonacci Frobenius Character — GL_1 Langlands base case
sigma^p(1,0)[e] == (5/p) mod p for all primes p != 5.

The QA sigma-orbit at prime p computes the Frobenius element of
Gal(Q(sqrt(5))/Q) directly from discrete orbit iteration — no LMFDB
lookup. F_p ≡ (5/p) mod p verified for 94 primes ≤ 500 (45 split,
49 inert) plus 5 large-prime spot-checks via matrix fast-path.

Positions the chain: [386] (ring classification) → [394] (orbit
computes GL_1 character) → [390] (GL_2 HMF eigenvalue symmetry).
The missing oracle step between structural identification and the
Galois eigenvalue story.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
feat(exp): NIST SP 800-22 Rev 1a validation of FibonacciCTR (App 8)
10/10 tests PASS for FibonacciCTR mod 31, mod 1009, mod 100003, and
os.urandom reference. Orbit-only control fails 8/10 tests, confirming
BLAKE2s provides the statistical uniformity while QA provides the
analytically proved period guarantee (π(p) = Pisano period).

Also fixes a critical nistrng 1.2.3 bug: BinaryMatrixRankTest stores
a view of the input bits array and runs Gaussian elimination in place,
corrupting every test that runs after it in the battery. Fix: pass a
fresh copy to each test call rather than using run_all_battery.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Tests π(3)=8-resonance prediction on 49 Aberdeenshire recumbent stone
circles (Wikipedia, independent of Thom 1962 derivation set).

Results:
  π(3) enrichment: NOT confirmed in RSCs (z=-0.41, p=0.66)
  Fibonacci trend: directionally positive but not significant (z=+1.22)

Diagnosis: RSCs cluster 20-30 MY; π(3) signal in Thom 1962 is partly
driven by small circles (8,16 MY) and mode at 40=5×8 MY absent here.
Honest result — prediction holds in derivation set, MIXED in RSCs.
Thom 1967 general catalog (not accessible online) is the right target.

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[393]): QA BEDA Hard Problem Analysis
Three security analyses: BEDA-toy (keyspace=192, O(192) brute force,
zero security), BEDA-DLP (classically hard when pi(p) has large prime
factor, NOT post-quantum — Shor applies to all cyclic groups of known
order), BEDA-LWE (PQC path via Module-LWE over Z[phi]^k with k>=128,
not yet instantiated). 7 checks PASS + 8 fixtures (7 PASS, 1 designed
FAIL). Sources: Stinson (2006), Boneh & Shoup (2023), Wall (1960).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[392]): QA φ-adic Period Tower (Cassini-Witt)
Cert [392]: QA Phi-adic Period Tower (Cassini-Witt).

CLAIM: (A) N(σ^k(1,0)) = (−1)^(k+1) for all k (Cassini via norm
multiplicativity of cert [391]); (B) π(p^n) = p^(n−1)·π(p) for
inert {3,7,13,17}, split {11,19,29,41}, ramified {5}, n=1..3+;
(C) Witt multiplier = p exactly for all tested primes;
(D) carries (F_π/p, (F_{π+1}−1)/p) mod p non-zero for {3,7,11,19,29}
— no Wall-Sun-Sun prime in test set.

7 checks PASS + 8 fixtures (7 PASS, 1 designed FAIL).
Meta-ledger: 339 entries. Expected hash bootstrapped.

Deepens the ℤ[φ] real multiplication chain: [391]→[392].
Primary sources: Wall (1960), Ireland & Rosen (1990), Serre (1979).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[391]): QA σ-Norm Negation — σ IS φ-multiplication on ℤ[φ]
Certifies the algebraic foundation underlying certs [385]–[390]:
σ(a,b) = (a+b,a) IS multiplication by φ on ℤ[φ] under (a,b) ↔ a·φ+b.

Since N(φ) = −1 (algebraic norm from ℚ(√5)/ℚ), σ is a norm-negating
endomorphism: N(σ(a,b)) = −N(a,b) where N(a,b) = b²+ab−a².

Geometric trace:
- split/ramified primes: norm-zero locus mod p = σ-eigenline union exactly
- inert primes: norm form is anisotropic (no norm-zeros)

Additional claims: disc(N)=5=disc(ℚ(√5)/ℚ); σ^k(1,0)=(F_{k+1},F_k) for
all k (orbit of φ traces the Fibonacci sequence because σ=×φ and
φ^k=F_k·φ+F_{k-1}).

7 checks (all PASS), 8 fixtures (7 PASS, 1 designed FAIL). Verified on
split/ram primes {5,11,19,29,41,59,61,71} and inert {2,3,7,13,17,23,43,47}.
Bootstraps expected_hash.json + updates ledger (338 families).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[390]): QA HMF Galois Conjugation Symmetry — Hecke eigenvalue permutation
Cert [390] certifies that the Galois automorphism tau: phi->psi=1-phi of
Q(sqrt(5))/Q acts identically on three structures simultaneously:
  (A) permutes Galois-conjugate HMFs f1=2.2.5.1-31.1-a <-> f2=2.2.5.1-31.2-a
  (B) permutes the two prime ideals above each split rational prime p
  (C) permutes QA Fibonacci roots r1 <-> r2 (since r1+r2≡1 mod p always)

Hecke eigenvalue claims verified against LMFDB for 34 prime ideals N<=151:
- inert primes (p≡±2 mod 5): a_{f1}(p) = a_{f2}(p)
- split primes (p≡±1 mod 5): multiset {a_{f1}(p1),a_{f1}(p2)} = {a_{f2}(p1),a_{f2}(p2)}
- ramified p=5: equal; Weil bound holds throughout

5 checks (INERT_EQUAL/RAMIFIED_EQUAL/SPLIT_PERMUTED/QA_SWAP_MIRRORS_GALOIS/WEIL_BOUND),
all PASS. 7 fixtures (6 PASS, 1 designed FAIL at split prime p=11 ordered swap).
Extends [388] (split eigenspace), [386] (prime classification), [385] (real multiplication).

Also bootstraps qa_kg_determinism_cert_v1/expected_hash.json and
updates _meta_ledger.json after FAMILY_SWEEPS extension.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[389]): QA Witt Tower Orbit Refinement — period law at level p²
Certifies that sigma-orbit periods at level p² are exactly
{1} ∪ Periods_nt(p) ∪ p·Periods_nt(p): every non-trivial level-p period
persists AND appears multiplied by p; period-p (zero) does NOT lift.
Corollary: inert/split-equal → 3 tiers at p², split-unequal → 5 tiers —
finer discriminant than level-p test. Hecke interpretation: k→p·k map is
local level-raising; k-period orbits at p² outside p·Periods_nt are old-part
(embedded from level p); p·k orbits are new-part. Closes the local picture:
[385] RM, [386] prime classification, [387] Witt J at inert p,
[388] eigenspace at split p, [389] tower p→p² for all types.
5 checks, 6 fixtures (5 PASS + 1 designed FAIL).

Sources: Wall (1960) doi.org/10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541;
Serre (1979) doi.org/10.1007/978-1-4757-5673-9; Neukirch (1999)
ISBN 978-3-540-65399-8; Ireland & Rosen (1990) ISBN 978-0-387-97329-6.

Also: refresh expected_hash.json + _meta_ledger.json after [389] added to
FAMILY_SWEEPS.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[388]): QA Split Prime Orbit Geometry — intermediate period for split primes
Certifies that sigma(a,b)=(a+b mod p, a) on F_p x F_p has three orbit
periods {1, ord_min, pi(p)} for split primes with unequal root orders
{11,19,29,31}; period-ord_min orbits = eigenspace {(r_min*c,c): c in F_p*}
of sigma for min-order root; inert primes {3,7,13,17} have only two periods
— intermediate period is a split-prime signature of non-locality. Equal-order
case (p=41, both roots order 40) also certified: only {1, 40}.
5 checks, 6 fixtures (5 PASS + 1 designed FAIL).

Sources: Wall (1960) doi.org/10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541; Neukirch (1999)
ISBN 978-3-540-65399-8; Ireland & Rosen (1990) ISBN 978-0-387-97329-6.

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[387]): QA Witt Carry Sub-Orbit Invariant — Teichmuller coset structure
Certifies the algebraic mechanism behind the 3 Cosmos sub-orbits:
Z[phi]/(9) = W_2(GF(9)) decomposes as T x U_1 (Teichmuller order-8 ×
1-unit (Z/3)^2 order-9). The Witt carry J(a,b) = coset_idx((b,a))
is constant on each sigma-orbit and classifies all three sub-orbits.
T-class sequence (mod-3 image) is identical across all 3 orbits —
the distinction lives entirely in the 1-unit / Witt carry layer.
5 checks, 6 fixtures (5 PASS + 1 designed FAIL).

Sources: Serre (1979) Local Fields; Neukirch (1999); Wall (1960).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[386]): QA Inert/Split/Ramified Prime Classification for Z[phi]
Certifies the full prime splitting trichotomy of Z[phi]=O_{Q(sqrt(5))}:
inert (p mod 5 in {2,3}) / split (p mod 5 in {1,4}) / ramified (p=5).
5 checks pass, 6 fixtures (5 PASS + 1 designed FAIL). Explains algebraically
WHY cert [385]'s 3-orbit structure exists (inert => local ring) and when
it fails. Key result: GF(9)^* = <phi>, so the 8-cycle Satellite IS phi
cycling through all of GF(9)^*.

Sources: Neukirch (1999); Wall (1960) doi.org/10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541;
Ireland & Rosen (1990).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
feat(cert:[385]): QA Orbit Prime Ideal Filtration — Z[phi]/(9) ↔ Nikolaev real multiplication
Certifies that the QA mod-9 orbit partition {Cosmos, Satellite, Singularity}
coincides exactly with the prime ideal filtration of Z[phi]/(9) induced by
the unique inert prime (3) over 3 in Z[phi] = O_{Q(sqrt(5))}:

  Cosmos   (72 pairs)  ↔  (Z[phi]/9)^*      [units, |3 inert|]
  Satellite  (8 pairs)  ↔  (3)/(9) \ {0}    [ideal minus zero]
  Singularity (1 pair)  ↔  {0}               [zero element]

Five checks all PASS:
  C1 IRREDUCIBLE_MOD3  x^2-x-1 irreducible mod 3 => 3 inert => Z[phi]/(9) local
  C2 LOCAL_RING        |units|=72, |maximal ideal|=9
  C3 COSMOS_UNITS      72 Cosmos pairs = (Z[phi]/9)^* exactly
  C4 IDEAL_STRATA      8 Satellite pairs = (3)\{0}; (9,9) -> {0}
  C5 NIKOLAEV_RM       periods 24/8/1 = Pisano pi(9)/pi(3)/1 =>
                       real multiplication by Z[phi] (Nikolaev 2024 Lemma 3.3)

Sources: Nikolaev (2024) doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2412.09148 (Lemma 3.3);
         Wall (1960) doi.org/10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Pisano periods).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3

Added features (all integer arithmetic):

  - 1x31 / 31x1 directional strip means + H-V anisotropy (31-pixel scale):
    corn row spacing creates detectable directionality at ~93m strips

Previously 40 pairs INDISTINGUISHABLE; all crossed 0 with new features.

  3997 integer features

  10 classes at 100%; 0 spectral-limit errors

author: Player3
content:
perf+feat([291]): vectorised Gini + random-subspace ensemble → 98.8% Indian Pines
best_split rewritten with numpy cumsum over all split positions simultaneously
(~50x faster than Python inner loop).  predict_all uses stack-based batch routing.

Random-subspace forest (31 trees, 40% feature subspace, seed=42):
  - Single tree:  91.3% test accuracy, 223 errors, 194 variance / 29 sensor-limit
  - Ensemble:     98.8% test accuracy,  30 errors,   0 variance / 30 sensor-limit
  - 8 classes hit 100%; Corn/Soy confusion drops from 220 to 5 errors

Three-tier certificate taxonomy introduced:
  SEPARABLE (70): single-feature gap >= 0 -> zero errors guaranteed
  RESOLVABLE (20): no single feature but ensemble discriminates
  INDISTINGUISHABLE (30): sensor limit -- residual 30 errors require LiDAR/multi-temporal

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
experiments(qa_eeg): QA-native consecutive-quantisation model — Fisher p=5.8e-19
Replace KMeans-based combined-2 with a Theorem NT-compliant pipeline:
amplitude samples quantised to {1,...,9} by percentile, consecutive (b,e)
pairs classified by exact modular orbit conditions (Singularity b=e=9,
Satellite 3|b∧3|e), z-scored against interictal baseline.

Result on 12 held-out CHB-MIT patients: mean ΔR²=+0.275 (SE 0.074),
Fisher χ²=143.3, p=5.8×10⁻¹⁹, 11/12 positive.

Update paper.tex: new §7 QA-Native Held-Out Validation, updated abstract
and title to reflect the cleaner, stronger result.
